question.text: Is day trading (squaring off intraday) permitted for institutional investors?

answer.text: No, no institutional investor shall be allowed to do day trading
Yes, it is permitted with additional margin

explanation.text: **The Logic:** No institutional investor shall be allowed to do day trading. All transactions are grossed for institutional investors at the custodian level. **The Trap:** Even though institutions trade on exchanges, their transactions must result in actual delivery - no intraday squaring off. **Cross-Exam Context:** This restriction prevents speculation by institutional investors who get preferential margin treatment. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Institutions must fulfil their obligations on a gross basis, ensuring actual delivery of securities.
